title: Define manipulative-spec schema + provenance/verification record
context: Every parametric manipulative in this project is generated from a versioned spec that encodes all dimensional, labelling, and print requirements. Without a machine-readable schema for those specs, parallel contributors will produce incompatible artifacts and the verification harness has no contract to check against. A provenance and verification record format is equally critical: it must capture who generated the file, which spec version was used, which tolerance checks passed, and whether a physical print test was performed.
objective: Author and publish a machine-readable JSON/TypeScript schema for the manipulative-spec format and a companion provenance/verification-record schema, so that every generated artifact is traceable and verifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema captures all required fields: manipulative family, nominal dimensions in mm, tolerance, paper size(s), colour mode(s), font reference, calibration bar specification, and relationship invariants (e.g. two halves == one whole).

Provenance/verification record schema captures: spec id + version, generator version, generation timestamp, dimensional-check result, relationship-check result, and physical-print-test record (date, printer model, measured dimensions, pass/fail).
Schemas are published with at least one valid example instance each.
Schemas are licensed CC-BY-4.0 and versioned (v1.0.0 or semver pre-release).
resources: PLAN.md (this repo) -- dimensional and tolerance requirements
tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide task for unit and tolerance definitions
output: A versioned schema file (schema/manipulative-spec.schema.json) and a provenance record schema (schema/provenance-record.schema.json), each with an example instance, licensed CC-BY-4.0.
